title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. transsilvanicum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. transsilvanicum, also known as wild angelica, is a subspecies of the Heracleum sphondylium plant, a biennial or perennial herb native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of wild angelica include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Wild angelica can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
